hyperedges.label: Browser QA Stack (agent-browser primary, Playwright fallback)
Security-Sensitive Review Chain (RLS + webhook verification + audit gate)
Three Loading Behaviors Documented Across Multiple Docs

nodes.label: How This Structure Works — Mental Model
nodes.norm_label: how this structure works — mental model

nodes.label: The Golden Loop
nodes.rationale: Never code without a written plan; a phase isn't done until tests are green AND you've seen it run; ship something runnable each phase — the repeating cycle Plan→Build→Test→Demo→Verify→Update tracker→Confirm→Next phase keeps every phase both proven and visible.

nodes.label: 3-Layer Tracking System
nodes.rationale: Three separate views of the same work (PLAN.md vision, per-phase spec, PROGRESS.md status) are kept in sync so build status is never a mystery — any session, machine, or teammate resumes instantly from committed files rather than memory.

nodes.label: Definition of Done (DoD)
nodes.rationale: Each phase lists its exit criteria before work starts so 'done' is objective, not a vibe — enforced by /arc-phase-done which refuses to close a phase without green tests, a live demo, and an updated tracker.

nodes.label: Walking Skeleton (Phase 0)
nodes.rationale: Phase 0 wires the whole flow end-to-end on fake data before adding depth, de-risking the architecture on day one; phases are then ordered by risk, not ease, so the part that could kill the project is proven earliest.

nodes.label: Offline-First / Interface+Fake+Real Pattern
nodes.rationale: An interface + fake + real implementation for every external dependency enables a stub mode with no keys or network — fast dev, deterministic tests, free CI — while real integrations stay optional until needed.

nodes.label: Change Intake
nodes.rationale: A mid-build change, idea, or suggestion — even the AI's own — is never coded ad-hoc; it is routed through triage (current-phase note / new phase spec / ADR / bug fix) and the tracker updated first, so the tracker never drifts out of sync with the code.

nodes.label: Quality & Safety Gates
nodes.rationale: Definition of Done, tests-per-feature, golden/eval sets, a security & privacy pass, cost/budget ceilings, observability, idempotency/resumability, and a rollback/kill switch are treated as non-skippable gates rather than optional polish.

nodes.label: Three Loading Behaviors
nodes.rationale: Golden rule: if something is a guideline, it belongs in CLAUDE.md (advisory, can be forgotten in long sessions); if it must always/never happen, it becomes a hook (deterministic, cannot be forgotten); if it's a capability, it loads on demand via command/agent/skill/rule to keep the context window lean.

nodes.label: Five-Layer Memory Stack
nodes.rationale: File-based, git-versioned memory (CLAUDE.md+rules, PLAN.md+ADRs, PROGRESS.md+phases, session-log.md) plus Claude's machine-local native auto memory is the verified best practice for per-project coding — diffable, portable, zero infra — with vector/graph memory servers reserved for cross-project or team memory pools.

nodes.label: Stripe Webhook Idempotency
nodes.rationale: Webhook events are idempotent by storing processed event IDs, so Stripe's at-least-once delivery and retries can never double-apply an entitlement change.

nodes.label: RLS Default-Deny Pattern
nodes.rationale: Row Level Security defaults to deny, with explicit per-table policies (select/insert/update/delete) scoped to auth.uid() — least-privilege data access enforced at the database layer regardless of application-layer bugs.

nodes.label: Design System Reuse Convention
nodes.rationale: Reuse components/ui/* primitives before building new ones, keep styling to shared Tailwind tokens (no inline hex), and require loading/empty/error states on every async view — consistency and robustness enforced as a convention rather than left to each component.

nodes.label: Deploy Flow with pre-deploy-guard
nodes.rationale: The pre-deploy-guard hook re-runs the test suite before vercel --prod and physically blocks the deploy on failure, so broken code cannot reach production even if a human forgets to check.

nodes.norm_label: arc vs gstack division of labor
